reviews.author: Al Viro
reviews.summary: Reviewer Al Viro suggested unifying the path_put() calls in nfsd_cross_mnt() by providing an example diff, which would simplify the code and improve maintainability.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch is reasonable and has already been taken into another tree, but suggested sending an official patch for cleanup.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks reasonable. Chuck has already taken this patch into his tree, but
we could do a cleanup on top. Want to send us an "official" patch?

Thanks,
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Al Viro
reviews.summary: Reviewer Al Viro noted that the patch is completely untested and only compiles, requesting additional testing before approval.

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: reviewer stated that any of the patches in this series could be replaced by existing patches in the nfsd-testing branch
reviews.raw_body:
We can replace any of the patches already in nfsd-testing, it's a topic
branch.


-- 
Chuck Lever

2026-06-01.patch_summary: This patch series addresses medium-severity bugs in the NFS server code, including memory leaks and refcounting issues that can be triggered remotely. The patches fix problems such as file handle verification, path reference management, and layout fence worker synchronization. The changes aim to prevent potential buffer overruns and improve overall stability of the NFS server.
